space.$description: Primitive spacing scale for the River Romney design system. Raw values only — use semantic spacing tokens (spacing.*) in UI code instead. The scale runs from hairline micro-spacing (2px) to full-bleed layout spacing (128px). All steps from 2xs through 5xl are referenced by at least one semantic token except 3xs (intentional — optical-only).

3xs.$description: Hairline. For optical adjustments, icon-to-label nudges, and micro-corrections that are not real spacing they are just making something look right. Not for padding or margin. Intentionally unmapped from semantic tokens.

2xs.$description: Micro spacing. Inline icon-to-text margins, gap between a badge and its label, tight internal element nudges. The smallest value used for genuine spacing (not just optical correction). Referenced by: spacing.micro.

xs.$description: Small spacing. Compact internal element padding the tightest spacing that reads as intentional breathing room. Referenced by: spacing.tight.

sm.$description: Form-level spacing. Gaps between list items, input internal padding, spacing between closely related inline elements. Referenced by: spacing.inline.

md.$description: Default spacing. The baseline unit if there is no reason to go tighter or looser, this is the value. Standard internal padding for most components. Referenced by: spacing.element.

lg.$description: Comfortable spacing. Card padding, gap between a label and its associated content, space between sibling components. Referenced by: spacing.component.

xl.$description: Generous spacing. Grid column gaps, space between a heading and its content block, outer padding for container elements. Referenced by: spacing.group.

2xl.$description: Layout spacing. Section padding, gap between major layout regions. Referenced by: spacing.layout.

3xl.$description: Large section break. Vertical rhythm between major content sections on a page the beat between distinct page blocks that are not full hero-level breaks. Referenced by: spacing.block.

4xl.$description: Page-level spacing. Generous vertical separation between top-level page sections. Referenced by: spacing.page.

5xl.$description: Hero and full-bleed spacing. Maximum vertical space for hero sections, full-bleed dividers, and deliberate empty space used as a design element. Referenced by: spacing.section.
